channel.title: Site on MahdiKhadem
channel.description: Recent content in Site on MahdiKhadem

item.title: A greeting from the depths of the voiceless
item.description: Right now, as I write this, it has been 45 days since the internet was cut off. I am frustrated. The days do not pass. I cannot do anything. My morning routine has become scanning for new DNS resolvers to find something that lets me at least transfer 500 kilobytes so I can read my emails. Writing code, browsing the web, digging through GitHub, reading interesting papers from PubMed, or even installing a library with pip have become impossible wishes. My only consolation these days is being able to connect to the internet for a few seconds or minutes to receive a notification from Stijin, or an email requesting to review a manuscript (that I cannot even download the PDF file attached!!). Stijin has become a symbol of the scientific community for me: When I see the notification of his WhatsApp messages, I feel a rush of dopamine. But does it matter? The voices of us, the people of Iran, were cut with the push of a button. The issue is not whether our opinions matter — they never have. The issue is that we are no longer even allowed to have opinions. What should we say? Say it’s fine and accept this calamity that has befallen us? Or refuse to accept it? Do we even exist to confirm or deny? Do we even have the right to protest?

item.title: My Linear Model Thinks Getting Older Makes You Poorer (And Other Statistical Tragedies)
item.description:
So there I was, building a wage-prediction model like a responsible data scientist, when my Ridge regression dropped a truth bomb that shattered my worldview: According to my coefficients, every birthday makes you $0.31 poorer per hour.

I immediately canceled my upcoming birthday party and started reverse-aging exercises. Then I remembered: I had fallen into the classic trap of misinterpreting linear model coefficients. Again.

Let me save you from similar existential crises with a lighthearted tour of coefficient interpretation pitfalls—complete with the statistical equivalent of stepping on a Lego barefoot.

item.title: How to Build a Hugo Site
item.description:
This article offers a instruction that can be used in to build a Hugo Site.

1) Install Hugo with winget Link to heading

On Windows PowerShell:

winget install Hugo.Hugo.Extended

Hugo’s official Windows install docs explicitly list winget install Hugo.Hugo.Extended for the extended edition.

Quick check:

hugo version

2) Create a new site Link to heading 

hugo new site site_name

That’s the official CLI command for creating a new Hugo site directory.
